is an events center located in [Sioux City, Iowa](/wiki/Sioux_City,_Iowa "Sioux City, Iowa"). The center also includes the **Fleet Farm Arena**, formerly known as **Gateway Arena**, a multi-purpose arena. The center was announced in September 2000 and broke ground in April 2002. The center officially opened in December 2003. The arena has three spectator levels: one suite level and two general seating levels named the 100 level and the 200 level, respectively. Its official maximum capacity is 10,000. The center is owned by Sioux City and is operated by Spectra.

Tullis, Nate (May 1, 2002). ["Proud City Breaks Ground for Events Center"](https://www.siouxcityjournal.com/news/local/proud-city-breaks-ground-for-events-center/article_a97e7c76-8a71-5126-a75f-509546f18d0f.html). *[Sioux City Journal](/wiki/Sioux_City_Journal "Sioux City Journal")*. Retrieved November 19, 2011.

**Tyson Events Center** is an events center located in [Sioux City, Iowa](/wiki/Sioux_City,_Iowa "Sioux City, Iowa"). The center also includes the **Fleet Farm Arena**, formerly known as **Gateway Arena**, a multi-purpose arena. The center was announced in September 2000 and broke ground in April 2002. The center officially opened in December 2003. The arena has three spectator levels: one suite level and two general seating levels named the 100 level and the 200 level, respectively. Its official maximum capacity is 10,000. The center is owned by Sioux City and is operated by Spectra.

It is home to the [Sioux City Musketeers](/wiki/Sioux_City_Musketeers "Sioux City Musketeers") of the [United States Hockey League](/wiki/United_States_Hockey_League "United States Hockey League") (USHL) and the [Sioux City Bandits](/wiki/Sioux_City_Bandits "Sioux City Bandits") of the [National Arena League](/wiki/National_Arena_League "National Arena League") (NAL). The arena also hosts many college tournaments associated with the [NAIA](/wiki/National_Association_of_Intercollegiate_Athletics "National Association of Intercollegiate Athletics"), including the NAIA [Wrestling](/wiki/Wrestling "Wrestling") National Tournament, NAIA Women's [Volleyball](/wiki/Volleyball "Volleyball") National Tournament, and the Division II NAIA Women's National Basketball Tournament, which was won back-to-back in 2004 and 2005 by Sioux City's [Morningside College](/wiki/Morningside_University "Morningside University") Mustangs.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tyson_Events_Center&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In September 2000, it was announced that Sioux City would be developing an arena/event center to replace the [Sioux City Municipal Auditorium](/wiki/Sioux_City_Municipal_Auditorium "Sioux City Municipal Auditorium").[[5]](#cite_note-5) Plans were later expanded in December, and the project was formally announced that same year.[[6]](#cite_note-6) In 2001, [IBP, Inc.](/wiki/IBP,_Inc. "IBP, Inc.") and its parent, [Tyson Foods](/wiki/Tyson_Foods "Tyson Foods"), purchased the naming rights for the event center.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Additionally the arena inside the center's naming rights were purchased by [Gateway, Inc.](/wiki/Gateway,_Inc. "Gateway, Inc.")[[8]](#cite_note-8) The arena officially broke ground on April 30, 2002 with construction beginning shortly after.[[9]](#cite_note-9) The Tyson Events Center officially opened on December 17, 2003.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

In 2007, [Acer Inc.](/wiki/Acer,_Inc. "Acer, Inc.") acquired Gateway but retained the Gateway Arena naming rights until the rights expired in June 2019.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) On September 30, 2019, retail chain [Fleet Farm](/wiki/Fleet_Farm "Fleet Farm") took over as naming rights partner under an eight-year deal which would brand the arena as Fleet Farm Arena.[[13]](#cite_note-13) In 2018, the center, which was operated by Sioux City, went under management by Philadelphia-based venue management firm Spectra.[[14]](#cite_note-14) Spectra was acquired by [Oak View Group](/wiki/Oak_View_Group "Oak View Group") in November 2021 to form OVG360 and in the process took over the arena management.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## Hosted events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tyson_Events_Center&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Hosted events")]

### Wrestling

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tyson_Events_Center&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Wrestling")]

The site has hosted [World Wrestling Entertainment](/wiki/World_Wrestling_Entertainment "World Wrestling Entertainment") on numerous occasions, including a live un-televised event on September 25, 2004, followed by a televised *[SmackDown](/wiki/WWE_Friday_Night_SmackDown "WWE Friday Night SmackDown")* on May 17, 2005; and four televised [Raw](/wiki/WWE "WWE") programs on July 10, 2006, February 5, 2007, January 12, 2009 along with an [ECW](/wiki/Extreme_Championship_Wrestling "Extreme Championship Wrestling") taping the same night, and Raw on July 1, 2013. There was another non-televised show on August 16, 2009. It has also hosted [TNA Wrestling](/wiki/TNA_Wrestling "TNA Wrestling") Live on June 4, 2010. In December 2014, there was a SmackDown televised event featuring the Immortal Hulk Hogan acting as an authority figure for the night.

In 2016 there was a WWE Live event (Smackdown) non-televised event featuring AJ Styles vs Dean Ambrose for the WWE Championship, The Usos vs Ryhno and Heath Slater for the Smackdown Tag Team Championships, The Hype Bros and American Alpha vs The Ascension and the Vaudevillians, Kalisto vs Tyler Brezze, The Miz vs Apollo Crews, Natalya and Carmela vs Emma and Naomi, Kane vs Bray Wyatt and Luke Harper and Alexa Bliss vs Becky Lynch for the Smackdown Women's Championship.

Most recently on April 22, 2019, the arena hosted non-televised WWE Live: Smackdown. The event featured 7 Matches including Charlotte Flair defeating Bayley via her signature Figure 8, Roman Reigns defeating Elias, WWE Intercontinental Champion Finn Balor defeating Mustafa Ali. The main event featured WWE Champion Kofi Kingston defeating Randy Orton to retain thanks to assistance from Kevin Owens who gave Orton a Stone Cold Stunner while the referee was knocked out and Kingston finished with his signature Trouble in Paradise.

WWE was scheduled to return to the arena on December 17, 2019 for a RAW taping that would air on December 23, 2019 but was cancelled by WWE for “scheduling conflicts” but was actually due to low ticket sales.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

### Entertainment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tyson_Events_Center&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Entertainment")]

* It has hosted numerous concerts, by artists such as [Aerosmith](/wiki/Aerosmith "Aerosmith"), [Cheap Trick](/wiki/Cheap_Trick "Cheap Trick"), [Alan Jackson](/wiki/Alan_Jackson "Alan Jackson"), [Dolly Parton](/wiki/Dolly_Parton "Dolly Parton"), [Britney Spears](/wiki/Britney_Spears "Britney Spears"), [Taylor Swift](/wiki/Taylor_Swift "Taylor Swift"), [Trace Adkins](/wiki/Trace_Adkins "Trace Adkins"), [Kenny Chesney](/wiki/Kenny_Chesney "Kenny Chesney"), [Cher](/wiki/Cher "Cher"), [Rascal Flatts](/wiki/Rascal_Flatts "Rascal Flatts"), [Fleetwood Mac](/wiki/Fleetwood_Mac "Fleetwood Mac"), [Kiss](/wiki/Kiss_(band) "Kiss (band)"), [Nickelback](/wiki/Nickelback "Nickelback"), [311](/wiki/311_(band) "311 (band)"), [Neil Diamond](/wiki/Neil_Diamond "Neil Diamond"), [Carrie Underwood](/wiki/Carrie_Underwood "Carrie Underwood"), [Little Big Town](/wiki/Little_Big_Town "Little Big Town"), [Ozzy Osbourne](/wiki/Ozzy_Osbourne "Ozzy Osbourne"), [Rob Zombie](/wiki/Rob_Zombie "Rob Zombie"), [Buckcherry](/wiki/Buckcherry "Buckcherry"), [The Offspring](/wiki/The_Offspring "The Offspring"), [Brad Paisley](/wiki/Brad_Paisley "Brad Paisley"), [Miranda Lambert](/wiki/Miranda_Lambert "Miranda Lambert"), [Whitesnake](/wiki/Whitesnake "Whitesnake"), [Papa Roach](/wiki/Papa_Roach "Papa Roach") and [Creed](/wiki/Creed_(band) "Creed (band)").
* This venue has also hosted comedian [Larry the Cable Guy](/wiki/Larry_the_Cable_Guy "Larry the Cable Guy") on three occasions.
* It has also hosted Monster Truck Nationals shows.

### Other events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tyson_Events_Center&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Other events")]

President [George W. Bush](/wiki/George_W._Bush "George W. Bush") spoke here during his re-election campaign in 2004.
